content:
Have you ever taken medicine when you were sick? Antibiotics are special medicines that help fight bad germs called bacteria. But sometimes, these germs can become super strong and stop listening to the medicine. This is called antibiotic resistance.

How does this happen? Well, if people take antibiotics when they don’t really need them—like for a cold caused by a virus—the germs don’t get all the medicine and learn how to fight back. Also, if someone stops taking their medicine too soon, some germs survive and become stronger. Even using antibiotics on animals can help these strong germs spread.

That’s why it’s really important to take antibiotics only when a doctor says so and to finish all the medicine. If we don’t, the germs will keep getting smarter, and our medicines won’t work as well to keep us healthy!
